channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
item.description: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs), cybersecuri...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers exploit critical flaw in Ninja Forms WordPress plugin
item.description: A critical vulnerability in the Ninja Forms File Uploads premium add-on for WordPress allows uploading arbitrary files without authentication, which can lead to remote code execution. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI: Americans lost a record $21 billion to cybercrime last year
item.description: U.S. victims lost nearly $21 billion to cyber-enabled crimes last year, driven primarily by investment scams, business email compromise, tech support fraud, and data breaches, the Federal Bureau of In...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
item.description: Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited N-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
item.description: By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders as benign and return sensitive data to the attacker's server....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Snowflake customers hit in data theft attacks after SaaS integrator breach
item.description: Over a dozen companies have suffered data theft attacks after a SaaS integration provider was breached and authentication tokens stolen. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: US warns of Iranian hackers targeting critical infrastructure
item.description: Iranian-linked hackers are targeting Internet-exposed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley programmable logic controllers (PLCs) on the networks of U.S. critical infrastructure organizations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today....
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Max severity Flowise RCE vulnerability now exploited in attacks
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a maximum-severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-59528, in the open-source platform Flowise for building custom LLM apps and agentic systems to execute arbitrary code. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Russian State-Linked APT28 Exploits SOHO Routers in Global DNS Hijacking Campaign
item.description: The Russia-linked threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard) has been linked to a new campaign that has compromised insecure MikroTik and TP-Link routers and modified their settings to turn the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: [Webinar] How to Close Identity Gaps in 2026 Before AI Exploits Enterprise Risk
item.description: In the rapid evolution of the 2026 threat landscape, a frustrating paradox has emerged for CISOs and security leaders: Identity programs are maturing, yet the risk is actually increasing. According to...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Authorities disrupt router DNS hijacks used to steal Microsoft 365 logins
item.description: An international operation from law enforcement authorities in partnership with private companies has disrupted FrostArmada, an APT28 campaign hijacking local traffic from MikroTik and TP-Link routers...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access
item.description: A high-severity security vulnerability has been disclosed in Docker Engine that could permit an attacker to bypass authorization plugins (AuthZ) under specific circumstances. The vulnerability, tracke...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: RSAC 2026: How AI Is Reshaping Cybersecurity Faster Than Ever
item.description: Dark Reading's Kelly Jackson Higgins shares insights on the past, present, and future of cybersecurity after attending RSAC 2026 Conference....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Human vs AI: Debates Shape RSAC 2026 Cybersecurity Trends
item.description: As AI dominated RSAC 2026, CISOs and industry leaders debated its role in security, from agentic applications to the challenges of scaling human involvement in decision-making....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Lies, Damned Lies, and Cybersecurity Metrics
item.description: A panel of five C-suite leaders discuss how cybersecurity success is measured and why it isn't improving results....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Why Your Automated Pentesting Tool Just Hit a Wall
item.description: Automated pentesting tools deliver strong early results, then quickly plateau. Picus Security explains how the "PoC cliff" leaves major attack surfaces untested and creates a dangerous validation gap....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Focusing on the People in Cybersecurity at RSAC 2026 Conference
item.description: AI dominated the RSAC 2026 Conference and showed it's still humans in cybersecurity who matter most....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
item.description: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy botnet. "A purpose...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Recurring Credential Incidents
item.description: When talking about credential security, the focus usually lands on breach prevention. This makes sense when IBM’s 2025 Cost of a Data Breach Report puts the average cost of a breach at $4.4 million. A...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
item.description: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even take f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Flowise AI Agent Builder Under Active CVSS 10.0 RCE Exploitation; 12,000+ Instances Exposed
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Flowise, an open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: German authorities identify REvil and GandCrab ransomware bosses
item.description: The Federal Police in Germany (BKA) has identified two Russian nationals as the leaders of GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations between 2019 and 2021. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New GPUBreach attack enables system takeover via GPU rowhammer
item.description: A new attack, dubbed GPUBreach, can induce Rowhammer bit-flips on GPU GDDR6 memories to escalate privileges and lead to a full system compromise. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
item.description: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Axios Attack Shows Complex Social Engineering Is Industrialized
item.description: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
item.description: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Disgruntled researcher leaks “BlueHammer” Windows zero-day exploit
item.description: Exploit code has been released for an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw reported privately to Microsoft, allowing attackers to gain SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
